ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a generic cookie class; no specific dark chocolate fudge cookie subclass exists. Mapped to the nearest parent class FOODON_03301710 (cookie food product).
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 A0CKN covers biscuits and cookies as a category; no specific dark chocolate fudge cookie code exists. Mapped to nearest parent category.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for dark chocolate fudge cookies; omitted to preserve ontology honesty.

serving.natural_language_portion: A typical serving is one dark chocolate fudge cookie weighing approximately 30 g, providing around 143 kcal.

nutrient_source.title: Cookies, chocolate chip (USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy, NDB No. 18158)
nutrient_source.organization: USDA Agricultural Research Service — FoodData Central
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: The HPB Singapore source reports anomalously low macronutrient values for this product (energy 42 kcal/100g, protein 0g, fat 0.25g, carbohydrate 10.36g), which are inconsistent with any known dark chocolate fudge cookie formulation. These values are more consistent with a dilute beverage than a baked cookie. USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry for chocolate chip cookies (NDB 18158) was used as the primary nutrient reference for a baked dark chocolate cookie, providing energy ~476 kcal/100g, protein ~5.4g, fat ~21.3g, carbohydrate ~65.5g. The HPB-reported values are preserved in notes as the original source data but are flagged as anomalous. Saturated fat estimated from USDA chocolate chip cookie profile. Micronutrients (potassium, calcium, iron, magnesium, cholesterol) cross-referenced with HPB where plausible and USDA where HPB values appear anomalous.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement found for 'Signature Dark Chocolate Fudge Cookies' in the University of Sydney GI Database. GI of 44 is based on the closest analogous entry: chocolate chip cookies (GI ~44, glucose reference, University of Sydney GI Database / Atkinson et al. 2008, Diabetes Care 31:2281-2283). Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GL = (GI × carbohydrate_g per serving) / 100 = (44 × 19.65) / 100 ≈ 8.6, using 30g serving with 65.5g/100g carbohydrate (19.65g per serving).

hypertension.key_factor: High sodium (340 mg/100g), high sugar, high saturated fat, and negligible potassium relative to sodium load
hypertension.guidance: Individuals with hypertension should avoid dark chocolate fudge cookies due to their high sodium content (340 mg/100g), high added sugar, and saturated fat, all of which independently elevate blood pressure; the low potassium-to-sodium ratio further impairs vascular regulation.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High refined carbohydrate (65.5g/100g) and high sugar (35g/100g) with low dietary fibre (2.3g/100g)
type2_diabetes.guidance: Dark chocolate fudge cookies are high in refined carbohydrates and added sugar with minimal fibre, leading to rapid postprandial glucose elevation; individuals with type 2 diabetes should strictly limit or avoid consumption and monitor blood glucose carefully if consumed.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High saturated fat (8.1g/100g), high sugar (35g/100g), trans fat present, and high energy density (476 kcal/100g)
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: The combination of saturated fat, trans fat, high added sugar, and high energy density in dark chocolate fudge cookies raises LDL cholesterol and promotes atherogenic risk; individuals with cardiovascular disease should treat these cookies as an occasional indulgence and limit portion size strictly.

llm_training.natural_language_description: Dark Chocolate Fudge Cookies are ultra-processed baked confectionery items made from wheat flour, dark chocolate or cocoa powder, butter, sugar, and eggs. They are characterised by a dense, fudgy texture and an intensely rich chocolate flavour derived from a high proportion of dark chocolate or cocoa solids. The cookies are typically baked at moderate oven temperatures until set on the outside but remain soft and chewy in the centre. Nutritionally, they are energy-dense (approximately 476 kcal per 100g), high in refined carbohydrates (65.5g/100g), added sugars (35g/100g), and saturated fat (8.1g/100g), with moderate sodium (340 mg/100g). Dietary fibre is low at 2.3g/100g. The glycemic index is estimated at 44 (low-medium range based on analogous chocolate chip cookie data), but the high carbohydrate load per serving still produces a meaningful glycemic response. Clinically, these cookies are unsuitable for individuals managing type 2 diabetes, hypertension, or cardiovascular disease due to their high sugar, saturated fat, sodium, and energy content. Dark chocolate provides trace amounts of flavanols and iron, but these benefits are substantially offset by the overall nutritional profile of the cookie formulation. The product is sold under the 'Signature' brand in Singapore and is classified as a snack or dessert item. It contains gluten, dairy, and eggs, making it unsuitable for those with relevant allergies or intolerances.

confidence_reason: The HPB Singapore source data for this record contains anomalously low macronutrient values (42 kcal/100g, 0g protein, 0.25g fat, 10.36g carbohydrate) that are inconsistent with any known dark chocolate fudge cookie formulation and are more consistent with a dilute beverage or flavoured water. These values have been replaced with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy data for the closest analogous product (chocolate chip cookies, NDB 18158), which represents a defensible and approved secondary source. Confidence is therefore medium rather than high because an exact match for this specific branded product was not available in any approved primary source, and the original HPB data appears to contain a data entry error.
notes: ANOMALOUS SOURCE DATA WARNING: The original HPB input record reports energy 42 kcal/100g, protein 0.0g, fat 0.25g, carbohydrate 10.36g, sugar 9.94g, sodium 3mg/100g for a product named 'Signature Dark Chocolate Fudge Cookies'. These values are physically inconsistent with a baked cookie and are more consistent with a dilute sugar-sweetened beverage or flavoured water. The HPB data has been flagged as a likely data entry error (possibly a mislabelled or misassigned record) and has been superseded by USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy values for chocolate chip cookies as the closest approved-source analogue. The original HPB values are preserved here for audit purposes: energy 42 kcal, protein 0.0g, fat 0.25g, carbohydrate 10.36g, sugar 9.94g, sodium 3mg, dietary_fibre 0.0g, potassium 5mg, calcium 1mg, magnesium 0.0mg, iron 0.02mg, cholesterol 0.0mg. Zero-macro justification: The original HPB record reports protein_g as 0.0 and dietary_fibre_g as 0.0; these are not naturally zero for a baked cookie and are considered data entry artefacts. In the corrected USDA-based record, protein_g (5.4g) and dietary_fibre_g (2.3g) are non-zero as expected for a baked chocolate cookie. Vitamin C, vitamin A, B-vitamins (B1, B2, B3, B6, B12), folate, vitamin D, vitamin E, phosphorus, zinc, and selenium are set to -1 (data not available): detailed micronutrient breakdown for this specific product is not available in USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry 18158 at the level of individual B-vitamins and fat-soluble vitamins beyond what is reported; these fields are set to -1 to indicate data unavailability rather than confirmed zero. Monounsaturated fat (7.2g), polyunsaturated fat (4.5g), and trans fat (0.1g) are estimated from the USDA chocolate chip cookie fatty acid profile. The food_group has been reclassified from 'Mixed' to 'Snacks' as cookies are more accurately classified as snack/confectionery items. This is a Western/international product sold in Singapore under the 'Signature' brand; it is not a traditional Singaporean dish and therefore the multilingual naming requirements for Singaporean dishes (Chinese, Malay, Tamil, Hindi) do not apply. The product name 'Signature' appears to be a brand or product line name rather than a descriptor; the canonical food name has been normalised to 'Dark Chocolate Fudge Cookies'. Ontology match types corrected to 'closest_parent' (from 'broader') to conform to the allowed enumeration values ['closest_parent', 'exact', 'none'] as required by the validator.

improvement_actions_taken: Iteration 1: Identified anomalous HPB source data (42 kcal/100g for a cookie); flagged as likely data entry error.

Iteration 2: Replaced HPB nutrient values with USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy NDB 18158 (chocolate chip cookies) as closest approved-source analogue; documented substitution fully in notes and nutrient_source; corrected food_group from Mixed to Snacks; added zero-macro justification for original HPB zeros; added -1 documentation for unavailable micronutrients; verified aliases consistency; confirmed G6 not triggered as product is Western/international not Singaporean.

Iteration 3: Corrected ontology.foodon_match_type and ontology.foodex2_match_type from 'broader' to 'closest_parent' to conform to the allowed enumeration ['closest_parent', 'exact', 'none'] as required by the G5 validator. This resolves the REJECT issued in iteration 2 validator feedback.
veritas_qc.reviewer_summary: PASS — all hard gates passed and the record scored 8/8.
